summary: Regression-tested Razorpay integration after refactor: dispatch-based webhook + logging in auth.js logout(). All 13 pytest regression tests pass (webhook signature verify, dispatch routing for payment.captured/authorized/order.paid/payment.failed, unknown-event pass-through & recording, idempotency, /api/payments/verify HMAC, live order creation). Frontend /payment renders, Pay button opens real Razorpay modal on LIVE keys (no Test Mode ribbon).

TestWebhookDispatch: unknown event payment.dispute.created -> 200 + recorded in webhook_events PASS
TestWebhookIdempotency: replay same payment.captured -> paid_at unchanged, single webhook_events row PASS
TestCreateOrder: POST /api/payments/create-order returns real order_ id, amount=29900, currency=INR, key_id=rzp_live_* PASS

frontend_verification.rizzlab_console_errors: none (only harmless razorpay iframe Mixed-Content warnings + pre-existing 401s on unrelated resource — same as iteration_4)

critical_code_review_comments: Webhook refactor is clean: WEBHOOK_HANDLERS dispatch dict + per-event helpers (_handle_payment_captured, _handle_order_paid, _handle_payment_failed) + shared _apply_paid keeps idempotency logic centralized.
_verify_webhook_signature returns False when RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is empty, but the endpoint also raises 503 earlier for missing secret — good defense in depth.
Handler exceptions are logged and swallowed so Razorpay does not retry-flood; event is still recorded in webhook_events before dispatch, so replay/reconciliation is possible.
server.py is 602 lines — approaching the 700-line threshold; consider splitting payments/webhook logic into /app/backend/routers/payments.py in a future iteration (not blocking).

RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is still the dev placeholder 'whsec_dev_placeholder_replace_with_dashboard_value' — must be replaced with the real dashboard value before production webhook goes live (already flagged in prior iterations).

success_rate.frontend: 100% (up to modal open; deeper iframe steps not automatable per iteration_4 note)

seed_data_creation: Each test inserts its own unique TEST_wh@example.com payment doc + unique evt_test_* webhook_events row. Left in DB (no cross-worker cleanup) since xdist parallel workers would otherwise wipe each other's rows. Safe: identifiers are unique per run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite at /app/backend/tests/test_webhook_regression.py covers signature/dispatch/idempotency/verify/create-order. Runs under existing pytest.ini (-n 2 --dist loadscope). Reuse and extend for any future payment-flow changes. RAZORPAY_WEBHOOK_SECRET is still a dev placeholder — production webhook tests against real dashboard signature would need the real secret.
